sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Betis are in strong form, unbeaten in their last 5 matches with 3 wins and 2 draws, scoring consistently. Espanyol, on the other hand, have struggled recently, managing only 1 win in their last 5 matches with 2 draws and 1 loss, showing inconsistency in attack and defense.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, Real Betis have dominated with 2 wins, including a 2-1 victory away at Espanyol. Their historical edge (4 wins in last 5 matchups) suggests they have the upper hand in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams have similar points (12) and league positions (Espanyol 9th, Betis 8th), with balanced goals scored and conceded. Betis show slightly better attacking efficiency, while Espanyol rely on headers and crosses (ranked high in league).

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Giovani Lo Celso has been pivotal for Betis against Espanyol, averaging an 8.18 Sofascore rating in recent matchups. Espanyol may focus on aerial threats, but Betis’ ability to score late (as seen in H2H) could be decisive.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Espanyol and Real Betis ended with a narrow 1-0 lead for the home side at Livens Jean Louis in Cornella. Espanyol took the lead through Pol Lozano in the 15th minute with a well-taken right-footed strike. Despite the scoreline, both teams showed intent, with Real Betis holding a slight edge in possession and making more entries into the penalty area. However, Espanyol's defense held firm, restricting Betis to no shots on target, while the hosts capitalized on their opportunity to score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Espanyol displayed a balanced approach in the first half, managing to create several promising situations in the final third while also defending resolutely. Their aerial dominance was evident, winning a significant portion of duels in the air, which helped them maintain control during key moments. Real Betis, on the other hand, showed more attacking intent with frequent entries into dangerous areas and multiple possessions won in the final third. Despite this, their finishing let them down, as they couldn’t convert their territorial advantage into clear chances. Both teams earned an equal number of corners, indicating a competitive battle in set-piece situations, while disciplinary issues were evident with two yellow cards each.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Espanyol, Pol Lozano stood out not only for his goal but also for his overall involvement in midfield, winning tackles and interceptions. Javi Puado showed promise in attack with a high likelihood of creating danger, while Clemens Riedel was solid at the back, contributing with clearances and an assist. On the Real Betis side, players like Giovani Lo Celso and Pablo Fornals were instrumental in building play with key passes and creative contributions. Cucho Hernández also posed a threat in the final third, though he couldn’t find the decisive touch. Both teams will look to their key figures to unlock the game further in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum and patterns
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Espanyol start strongly with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes, building pressure that eventually led to their goal. Their momentum was particularly high around the middle of the half, showcasing sustained periods of dominance in the opposition half. Real Betis, however, responded with a shift in momentum after the goal, gaining control and pushing forward with notable attacking intent towards the latter stages of the half. Their persistence in creating pressure was clear, especially in the closing minutes, though they lacked the final product to level the score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Real Betis are likely to come out with renewed vigor as they chase an equalizer. Their higher expected goals in the first half suggest they have the potential to convert chances if they can sharpen their finishing. Espanyol will aim to protect their lead, potentially focusing on counter-attacks to exploit spaces left by a pushing Betis side. Given Betis’ frequent penalty area entries, Espanyol’s defense, led by players like Leandro Cabrera, will need to remain compact. Tactical adjustments could play a significant role, with Betis possibly introducing more attacking substitutes to break down Espanyol’s resilience.
